messages.content:
Write a query that calculates annual salary from a table named employee with the following columns: - FullName ,Title 
,StartDate, MonthlySalary In the result set include columns for FullName, Title, StartDate, and AnnualSalary

To calculate the annual salary, we need to multiply the monthly salary by 12.

Here's the query to achieve this:

```sql
SELECT  
    FullName,
    Title,
    StartDate,
    MonthlySalary,
    MonthlySalary * 12 AS AnnualSalary
FROM 
    employee;
```

The query retrieves data from the "employee" table and selects columns FullName, Title, StartDate, MonthlySalary, and calculates the annual salary by multiplying MonthlySalary by 12. The result set will include these columns.

In T-SQL, which statement is used to modify existing data within a table? 1
• DELETE
• UPDATE
• INSERT
• ALTER TABLE
which iption is currect

The correct option is UPDATE. 

In T-SQL, the UPDATE statement is used to modify existing data within a table. The UPDATE statement allows you to specify the table you want to update, set new values for specific columns, and define the conditions that determine which rows should be updated.

In T-SQL, which statement is used to remove specific rows of data from a table? 1
• TRUNCATE TABLE
• CLEAR
• DELETE
• REMOVE ROW choose the currect option

The correct option is DELETE.

In T-SQL, the DELETE statement is used to remove specific rows of data from a table. With the DELETE statement, you can specify the table you want to delete rows from and define the conditions that determine which rows should be deleted. This statement allows you to selectively remove data from a table based on your criteria.

n T-SQL, which statement is used to modify existing data within a table? 1
• DELETE
• UPDATE
• INSERT
• ALTER TABLE choose the currect option

The correct option is UPDATE.

In T-SQL, the UPDATE statement is used to modify existing data within a table. You can use the UPDATE statement to change the values of specific columns in one or more rows of the table. It allows you to specify the table you want to update, set new values for specific columns, and define the conditions that determine which rows should be updated.

Which mathematical function is used to round a number down to the next lowest integer? 1
• DOWN()
• SMALLINT()
• FLOOR()
• LOWER()

The correct option is FLOOR().

In SQL, the FLOOR() function is used to round a number down to the next lowest integer. It takes a numeric value as input and returns the largest integer that is less than or equal to the input value. The FLOOR() function is commonly used to truncate decimal places and obtain whole numbers.

The correct option is:

```
UPDATE o
SET OrderStatus = 'Shipped'
FROM Orders o
INNER JOIN Customers c ON o.CustomerID = c.CustomerID
WHERE c.City = 'New York';
```

In this T-SQL statement, we are using the UPDATE statement with an INNER JOIN to update the OrderStatus column in the Orders table. We join the Orders table with the Customers table on the CustomerID column. Then, we specify the condition `WHERE c.City = 'New York'` to filter out orders placed by customers from New York. By using the alias "o" for the Orders table, we can set the OrderStatus column to 'Shipped' for the matched rows from the join result.

Why would you add a WHERE clause to a query? 1
• to tell the query what table you want to pull data from 
• to filter the results to a subset of records 
• to define the specific columns of data you want to return.
• to specify the database the query should execute on

The correct option is:

• to filter the results to a subset of records.

Adding a WHERE clause to a query allows you to specify conditions that the rows must meet in order to be included in the result set. By including a WHERE clause, you can filter the results and retrieve only the records that match the specified criteria. This is useful when you want to narrow down the data and focus on a specific subset of records that meet certain conditions.

Which of the following statements is true about the query below? 1
SELECT Title, Author, 'Book' FROM Inventory.
• It will return an unnamed column with the word "Book" in every row. 
• Text in single quotes within the SELECT clause will be returned as an attribute of each row in the results.
• It will return rows alphabetized by title. 
• It will return the Title, Author, and Book columns from the Inventory table. 
• It will return just the Book items from the Inventory table.

The correct statement about the query is:

• Text in single quotes within the SELECT clause will be returned as an attribute of each row in the results.

In the query `SELECT Title, Author, 'Book' FROM Inventory`, the text 'Book' is placed within single quotes. This signifies a literal string value in the SELECT clause rather than a column name. As a result, the query will return an unnamed column with the word "Book" in every row of the result set. This column will have the same value for every row.

: select count(empid), dept,salary from mr_data where namee = XYZ and salary >400 group 
by dept, salary, empid having avg(salary)>50000; 2
Error: Msg 207, Level 16, State 1, Line 25 Invalid column name 'XYZ'.
What can be wrong in the above query. Also, answer the right query for the same

The error message "Invalid column name 'XYZ'" suggests that there is no column named 'namee' in the 'mr_data' table.

To fix the query, you can try removing the condition "where namee = XYZ" or check if the column name is correct. 

Here's the corrected query without the invalid condition:

```sql
SELECT COUNT(empid), dept, salary
FROM mr_data
WHERE salary > 400
GROUP BY dept, salary, empid
HAVING AVG(salary) > 50000;
```

This query will count the number of employees (empid) in each department (dept) with a salary greater than 400. It then groups the result by dept, salary, and empid. Finally, it applies a having clause to filter only the groups with an average salary greater than 50000.

The FORMAT() function can be used to return dates in what data type? 1
• DateTimeOffset 
• Numeric 
• String 
• SmallDateTime

The correct option is String.

In T-SQL, the FORMAT() function is used to format and return data as a string. It allows you to specify a format pattern to convert the input data to a specific string representation. The FORMAT() function is commonly used to format dates, numbers, and other data types into human-readable strings.

The comparison operator !> is equivalent to _____. 1
• <=
• <>
• <
• <+

The comparison operator !> is equivalent to < or <=. 

To be more specific, the !> operator is a negation of the greater than operator (>), which means it checks if a value is less than or equal to another value. So, !> is equivalent to <= when used in a comparison.

Write a query to display the customer details like firstname, latsname,city,state,age of the customer who lives in the
city with names starting with letter S.

To display the customer details like firstname, lastname, city, state, and age of the customers who live in a city with names starting with the letter 'S', you can use the following query:

```sql
SELECT firstname, lastname, city, state, age
FROM customer
WHERE city LIKE 'S%'
```

In this query, we use the SELECT statement to retrieve the specified columns (firstname, lastname, city, state, age) from the customer table. The WHERE clause is used to filter the result by specifying the condition `city LIKE 'S%'`, which means the city name should start with the letter 'S'. The `%` is a wildcard character that allows any number of characters to follow the 'S'.
Query the customer count where the favoritedish lies between 10 to 20.

To query the count of customers where the favoritedish lies between 10 to 20, you can use the following query:

```sql
SELECT COUNT(*) as customer_count
FROM customer
WHERE favoritedish BETWEEN 10 AND 20;
```

In this query, we use the SELECT COUNT(*) to calculate the count of customers satisfying the specified condition. The WHERE clause is used to filter the results where the favoritedish column value is between 10 and 20. The result is returned as a single value under the alias "customer_count".
